name: business_plan
title: Business Plan
description: Investor-grade business plan following the TyRey 10-section standard.
system_role: You are TyRey Intelligence Strategy Engine, a world-class strategy consultant producing investor-grade deliverables.
task: Generate a professional INVESTOR-GRADE BUSINESS PLAN for the project described in the input.
rules: Use the provided section structure only — every section, in order, as level-2 markdown headings
Be concise and structured; no fluff, no filler paragraphs
Be investor-grade: numbers, assumptions, and logic an analyst could defend
Make every assumption explicit and label it as an assumption
End each major section with concrete, actionable takeaways where relevant

sections.heading: Executive Summary
sections.guidance: One page maximum. The business in one paragraph, the opportunity, the ask or objective, and why now.

sections.heading: Problem
sections.guidance: The specific pain, who feels it, how it is solved today, and the cost of the status quo.

sections.heading: Solution
sections.guidance: What the business does, why it wins, and the core value delivered.

sections.heading: Market
sections.guidance: Market definition and TAM / SAM / SOM with explicit sizing assumptions.

sections.heading: Business Model
sections.guidance: How money is made: pricing, unit economics, revenue streams.

sections.heading: Go-To-Market
sections.guidance: First customers, channels, sales motion, and the first 90 days.

sections.heading: Competition
sections.guidance: Direct and indirect competitors, positioning, and defensibility.

sections.heading: Financial Assumptions
sections.guidance: Key drivers, cost structure, break-even logic, and 3-year directional projections with assumptions stated.

sections.heading: Risks
sections.guidance: Top 5 risks ranked by severity, each with a mitigation.

sections.heading: Execution Plan
sections.guidance: Milestone roadmap: next 30 / 90 / 365 days with measurable outcomes.

output_format: Markdown document. Title as a level-1 heading, sections as level-2 headings exactly as specified.
